workflow.name: Process refund requests from Gmail using Shopify and Google Sheets
workflow.description:
## How it works  
This workflow monitors incoming Gmail messages for refund requests and uses AI to extract the order ID and reason. It then retrieves order details from Shopify to evaluate refund eligibility. Based on the order status, it automatically approves or routes the request for manual review. Customers and internal teams are notified accordingly, and all actions are logged in Google Sheets.

## Step-by-step  

- **Capture refund requests from email**  
  - **Gmail Trigger** – Detects unread emails with “Refund Request” in the subject.  
  - **AI Agent2** – Extracts order ID and reason using AI. 
    - **Groq Chat Model** – Provides the language model used by the AI agent.   
  - **Code in JavaScript** – Converts AI output into structured JSON.  


- **Fetch order details from Shopify**  
  - **Get an order** – Retrieves order information using the extracted order ID.  

- **Evaluate refund eligibility**  
  - **If** – Checks whether the order status is “Delivered” to allow auto-approval.  

- **Handle responses and logging**  
  - **Send a message to customer** – Sends approval confirmation if eligible.  
  - **Send a message to the team** – Notifies team for manual review cases.  
  - **Send a message to customer for "Pending" Status** – Updates customer about manual review.  
  - **Logs to Sheet** – Records all refund actions and details in Google Sheets.  

## Why use this?  

- Automates repetitive refund handling tasks and reduces workload  
- Improves customer experience with faster response times  
- Ensures consistent decision-making based on order status  
- Keeps a centralized log for auditing and reporting  
- Easily extendable with additional validation rules or integrations

parameters.message: ="Hi there,    Good news! We have reviewed your request for Order #{{ $('Code in JavaScript').item.json.order_id }} and your refund has been approved. You should see the credit in your account within 5-7 business days."
parameters.subject: =Refund Approved: Order #{{ $('Code in JavaScript').item.json.order_id }}

nodes.name: Send a message to the team

parameters.message: ="A refund request was received but could not be auto-approved.   Order ID: {{ $('Code in JavaScript').item.json.order_id }}  Reason: {{ $('Code in JavaScript').item.json.reason }}  Current Sheet Status:{{ $('Get row(s) in sheet').item.json.Status }} "
parameters.subject: =ACTION REQUIRED: Manual Refund Review (Order {{ $('Code in JavaScript').item.json.order_id }})

nodes.name: Send a message to customer for "Pending" Status

parameters.message: ="Hi, we've received your refund request. Because your order is currently marked as{{ $('Get row(s) in sheet').item.json.Status }}, a member of our team needs to review this manually. We will get back to you shortly."
parameters.subject: =Update on your Refund Request: Order #{{ $('Code in JavaScript').item.json.order_id }}

parameters.content:
# Automated Refund Request Handler

### How it works
This workflow automates refund handling from incoming Gmail messages. It detects emails with “Refund Request”, extracts the Order ID and reason using AI, and fetches real-time order data from Shopify. Based on the order status, it decides whether the refund can be auto-approved or requires manual review. Customers are notified automatically, and every action is logged into Google Sheets for tracking and analytics.

### Setup steps
1. Connect Gmail OAuth2 (set to production in Google Cloud).
2. Add Groq API key for AI processing.
3. Connect Shopify using an access token with read_orders permission.
4. Connect Google Sheets OAuth2 for logging.
5. Ensure your sheet contains columns: Date, Order ID, Customer Email, Reason, Outcome, Notes.

### Customization tips
- Add a date filter to allow refunds only within a specific time window.
- Replace Gmail alerts with Slack or Discord for faster team response.
- Extend logic to check payment status before approving refunds.

## Step 1: Intake & AI Extraction
Triggers on refund emails.
Extracts order ID and reason using AI.

## Step 2: Shopify Validation
Fetches order details from Shopify.
Used to verify refund eligibility.

## Step 3: Decision Logic
Checks if order is delivered.
Routes to auto-approve or manual review.

## Step 4: Notifications & Logging
Sends customer/team emails.
Logs outcome into Google Sheets.

main.node: Send a message to the team
Send a message to customer for "Pending" Status

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
